retry_reason: The block group either exists at the given bytenr or does not; retrying the same lookup without any intervening creation or allocation will produce the same result.
contract_summary: Get-wrapper that looks up a block_group by starting bytenr and acquires a reference on it. Returns a pointer to the block_group with its refcount incremented, or NULL if no block_group is found at that bytenr. The caller must check the return value and, if non-NULL, must release the reference with btrfs_put_block_group when done. No locks or RCU are required by the caller; the wrapper internally handles synchronization.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to look up a block group by bytenr and acquire a reference to it via block_group_cache_tree_search, which increments the refcount. The returned pointer is passed directly to the caller with an acquired reference. No other business logic exists in this function.
